Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

You will need to move absolute_path() function to debug.c instead. The reason is the strange library dependency are going on. We have libcfs.a, libptlctl.a, libcfsutil.a and then liblustreapi.so. Having absolute_path() in debug.c (libptlctl.a) depend on liblustreapi.so will cause strange dependency loops.

Patch Set 2:

Thanks, James.  I looked through the libcfs headers, would libcfs/include/libcfs/util/string.h be an appropriate place to put the declaration of absolute_path()?  If so, should the definition be moved from debug.c to libcfs/libcfs/util/string.c?

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 2:

Yes I like your suggestion.

parents.subject: LU-9410 ldiskfs: no check mb bitmap if flex_bg enabled

commit.subject: LU-8993 utils: Use absolute pathname for debug_daemon log file
commit.message:
LU-8993 utils: Use absolute pathname for debug_daemon log file

The lctl debug_daemon command is changed to always provide an
absolute pathname to the kernel.  The kernel code will return EINVAL
if the pathname does not begin with '/', leading to the confusing
error "Invalid argument". This patch allows the user to provide a
relative pathname to the command without generating this error.

The absolute_path function has been moved to string.c and renamed to
cfs_abs_path, so that it may be used by all utilities.

Signed-off-by: Steve Guminski <stephenx.guminski@intel.com>
Change-Id: I35af242bfcfcb9a56135aeabe0423e28e9634bab
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/25485
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
